Out-of-state donor to a Travis County race; identity not confirmed. Industry assigned by the categorize-by-employer fallback only.

name: Alvarez, Enrique

Searches of Austin PD, Travis County Sheriff's Office and Travis County constable rosters, and of retired-officer records, returned no Enrique Alvarez that could be tied to this zip; the only same-named Texas results (a Big Spring lawn-service owner) do not corroborate. Industry assigned by the categorize-by-employer fallback (retired law-enforcement career = Government), not by identifying the individual. The 37 federal donations with a 0.633 partisan lean could not be matched to a specific FEC contributor record.

name: Reyes, Angelica

Reyes, MBA, of Austin, TX is listed as Regional Coordinator at Catholic Charities USA — an exact match to the filed occupation 'Regional Coordinator' and employer 'Catholic Charities USA'. name: Mason, Barbara
evidence: The filing carries no zip, no occupation, no employer and no location — nothing to corroborate identity against. 'Barbara Mason' is a very common name with many Texas and national matches, and the single $103 gift to Ann Howard (2021-10-26) provides no distinguishing detail. FEC and Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist searches returned no record that can be attributed to this donor rather than to a same-named person; the 299 federal donations attached to this identity could not be independently verified to one individual. No employer fallback is available because no employer or occupation string exists.

name: Patterson, Tiffany
evidence: No occupation or employer was filed; only name and Austin 78759. At least two distinct Tiffany Pattersons are publicly active in that area — a licensed acupuncturist operating Texas Acupuncture Clinic at 11651 Jollyville Rd, Austin 78759 (a business, not residential, address), and a residential real-estate agent with Austin City Living who spent ~two decades in the nonprofit sector — plus a Tiffany Patterson on the YMCA of Austin staff. With no occupation string to break the tie, the single $103 gift to Ann Howard cannot be attributed to any one of them, so no industry is assigned. FEC PAC and Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist searches returned no attributable record.

name: Chello, Joseph
evidence: Record carries no zip, no occupation and no employer, and our FEC match count is 0. Web searches surface only a 'Joseph Chello' named as a hearing requestor in an unrelated 2026 TCEQ wastewater-permit docket and generic people-search aggregator entries (age 50-51), none of which can be tied to Austin/Travis County or to the 2021 gift to Ann Howard. No Austin-area professional profile, employer, or public bio found; identity not corroborated by zip, occupation, or donation pattern.

name: Levy, Jonathon
resolved_employer: The Leverage Learning Company LLC (learning-innovation / education consulting), Austin TX
evidence: Best match is Jonathon (David) Levy of Austin, principal of The Leverage Learning Company LLC since May 2016; previously LeveragePoint Innovations (2009-2016), Monitor Group (2003-2008), Harvard Business School (1999-2003) and Cornell University (1979-1999). The distinctive '-on' spelling plus an Austin location and a strongly Democratic federal giving pattern (fec_partisan_lean 0.98 across 132 donations) corroborate; the 78739 (Circle C, SW Austin) zip could not be independently confirmed, so confidence is capped at medium. Full profile/positions pages read; no board seats or policy-advocacy roles disclosed. Not on the Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry; no ideological-PAC contributions found on either side of the Israel/Palestine, gun, energy or defense spectrums.

name: Bryant, Samuel
evidence: Record has no zip, occupation or employer. A plausible but unconfirmed candidate exists: Samuel 'Sam' L. Bryant of Austin, president of Bryant Wealth Investment Group L.L.C., NAIFA member, board member of Capital Area United Way and Westminster Manor, past board member of Austin Community Foundation and Boys & Girls Club of Austin, president of the Mt. Sinai Christian Academy Advisory Committee, Army veteran and Tuskegee graduate - an Austin civic figure whose profile fits a 2021 gift to Travis County Pct. 1 Commissioner Jeff Travillion. However 'Samuel Bryant' is a very common name (400+ LinkedIn profiles), the record supplies no zip or occupation to corroborate, and the fec_donation_count of 272 is almost certainly a multi-person name collision. Not identified with confidence, so no industry assigned. No TEC lobbyist registration under this name; no ideological-PAC giving found.

name: Jones, Thomas
evidence: No zip, occupation, employer or location on the record, and 'Thomas Jones' is one of the most common names in the US - the attached fec_donation_count of 2,939 with a near-neutral 0.48 lean is a clear name-collision aggregate rather than one person. Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists (2021-2026) contain Austin lobbyists named James Adam Jones, Neal T. Jones Jr. and Noah M. Jones but no Thomas Jones. No individual could be corroborated by zip, occupation or donation pattern; no employer information exists to support the categorize-by-employer fallback.

name: Pouland, John
resolved_employer: Independent government-affairs/public-sector consultant, Austin area; formerly VP Government Affairs & Solutions at Philips and a similar role at Siemens; former GSA Greater Southwest Regional Administrator (retired from corporate roles)
evidence: John C. Pouland, attorney and longtime Texas Democratic figure: former Dallas County Democratic Party chairman, 1996 Democratic nominee in TX-05 against Pete Sessions, Executive Director of the Texas General Services Commission under Gov. Ann Richards, GSA Greater Southwest (Region 7) Regional Administrator 1995-96 and 1997-2001 under Clinton, then public-sector government-affairs executive at Siemens and Philips; now a consultant based in Austin. Corroborated by a 2022 Dallas officeholder campaign-finance filing listing contributor John Pouland at 345 Patriot Drive, matching the record's Buda, TX 78610 address, and by a 0.98 Democratic federal giving lean over 91 donations consistent with his party history. Full TXSES and GSA press-release bios read. Not listed on the Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry; no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun-rights, gun-control, oil & gas or defense-contractor PAC ties found.

affiliations.snippet: TXSES announced John Pouland - an Austin-based consultant, former VP for Government Affairs and Solutions at Philips and former GSA Southwest Regional Administrator - as a newly elected member of its board of directors.

affiliations.org: U.S. General Services Administration, Greater Southwest Region
affiliations.snippet: Pouland was named regional administrator for GSA's Greater Southwest Region, directing about 1,400 employees and a $394 million annual budget; he had earlier stepped down to run as the Democratic candidate in Texas's Fifth District against Pete Sessions.

name: Adams, Elisabeth

Adams in UT-Austin Classics, an Elizabeth Adams boudoir photographer, and an Elizabeth Adams at UT's Center for Electromechanics - all spell the name 'Elizabeth' and none is placed in 78754, so none can be matched to this 2017 gift to Commissioner Jeff Travillion. No TEC or City of Austin lobbyist registration found under this name; no ideological-PAC giving found. Identity not corroborated and no employer exists for the fallback.

name: Grosskopf, Karen
evidence: Could not confirm this specific person. The only Grosskopf household surfacing in West Lake Hills / 78746 is Bill (William) M. Grosskopf, a retired 40-year small-business manager and certified SCORE Austin mentor/VP tied to Grosskopf Holdings, LLC at 1500 Westlake Drive, Austin TX 78746; Karen may be a household member, but no public source names her as such, and no occupation, employer, bio, or board role for Karen Grosskopf herself was found. Her record shows 77 federal donations at a fully Democratic partisan lean (1.0), consistent with a prolific small-dollar ActBlue-style donor rather than an occupational or lobbying tie.

name: Barrera, Mari
resolved_employer: Nutter McClennen & Fish LLP — Senior Philanthropic Advisor, Private Client Department (also principal, Barrera Consulting)
evidence: Mari Brennan Barrera of Natick, MA 01760 — exact zip match — is a Senior Philanthropic Advisor in the Private Client Department at Boston law firm Nutter McClennen & Fish, with 25+ years advising family foundations on grantmaking (childhood hunger, affordable housing, social change leadership). FEC records corroborate the identity: small-dollar 2020 contributions to ActBlue and Biden for President from Natick 01760, employer listed as 'Barrera Consulting' / 'Nutter', matching this donor's small-dollar Democratic-side giving pattern. No Texas lobbyist registration and no ideological-PAC giving found; her out-of-state $103 to a Travis County race appears to be personal, not professional.

affiliations.snippet: Full bio lists her as former chair of Philanthropy Massachusetts, the statewide association of philanthropists and nonprofits.
Bio lists board membership at Project Bread, a Massachusetts anti-hunger organization, since 2020.
Bio lists her as former board chair of the Full Frame Initiative, a national social-change organization.
Bio lists her as serving a second term on the MetroWest Health Foundation's Natick grants panel.

name: DeAngelo, David
resolved_employer: DL DeAngelo Consulting, LLC — Principal (public affairs / political consulting); registered Texas lobbyist
evidence: David DeAngelo is the principal of DL DeAngelo Consulting, LLC, a public-affairs and political consulting firm registered at 12325 Zeller Ln, Austin TX 78753 — an exact match to this donor's zip. He appears on the Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists across multiple years (2023-2026), listed with occupation 'Political Consultant' at the same Zeller Ln address. Federal record shows 11 donations at a 0.996 Democratic partisan lean. This is a registered lobbyist making a small-dollar ($103) contribution to a sitting Travis County commissioner — exactly the influence-adjacent small-dollar pattern, not an ordinary resident gift.

affiliations.role: Registered lobbyist / political consultant (2023-2026 registration lists)

Firm site identifies DeAngelo as principal of a public affairs and political consulting practice that 'engineer[s] opportunities through relentless preparation' on clients' behalf.
